Company Overview: Aditya Auto Products is a global Tier 1 automotive supplier delivering innovative mechatronic solutions for the automotive industry. We specialize in high-performance motors and actuators for various applications, continuously advancing our capabilities through cutting-edge technology.

Role Overview: The AI/ML Acoustic Diagnostics Internship is a 6-month program aimed at solving real-world challenges using artificial intelligence and machine learning. Interns will work on developing an AI system capable of analyzing and classifying motor audio signals, ensuring superior quality and customer satisfaction. Key Responsibilities: - Develop and train AI/ML models to analyze audio signals from motors and classify them as OK or Not OK based on subjective noise criteria. - Design algorithms to identify specific noise issues such as Brush Noise, Bearing Noise, and Gearbox Noise. - Collaborate with quality and engineering teams to understand objective and subjective rejection criteria. - Create a robust dataset by collecting and preprocessing audio signals from motors in different operational states. - Evaluate model accuracy and improve performance through iterative training and validation. - Implement the solution in a user-friendly interface for operators to use on the production line. Who Should Apply: - Required Skills and Qualifications: Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Data Science, or a related field. - Strong understanding of machine learning techniques, especially in the field of audio signal processing. - Proficiency in Python and libraries like TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Scikit-learn. - Experience in feature extraction and audio signal analysis. - Ability to work with noisy datasets and implement robust preprocessing techniques. Preferred Skills: - Knowledge of digital signal processing (DSP) and acoustic diagnostics. - Familiarity with edge computing and deploying AI models in real-time systems. - Hands-on experience in data visualization tools and model explainability. - Understanding of automotive motor systems and components.
Eligibility: 60% or 6 CGPA In PG. This text belongs to the section: Eligibility.
Skills Required - Expertise in AI/ML - Expertise in Databases, SQL, PowerBI - Statistics NONE Skills Required "Expertise in AI/ML, Expertise in Databases, SQL, PowerBI, Statistics."

The Statistical Quality Data Scientist Internship is a 4-month program. The eligibility criteria for the AI/ML Acoustic Diagnostics Internship is 60% or 6 CGPA in PG. The eligibility criteria for the Industry 4.0 Data Analyst Internship and the Statistical Quality Data Scientist Internship is not mentioned in the provided text. The AI/ML Acoustic Diagnostics Internship is focused on developing an AI system capable of analyzing and classifying motor audio signals. The Industry 4.0 Data Analyst Internship is focused on transforming manufacturing processes through data-driven solutions. The Statistical Quality Data Scientist Internship is focused on analyzing warranty returns data to drive actionable insights and predictive models for improving customer satisfaction. The AI/ML Acoustic Diagnostics Internship requires skills in machine learning techniques, especially in the field of audio signal processing, proficiency in Python and libraries like TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Scikit-learn, and experience in feature extraction and audio signal analysis. The Industry 4.0 Data Analyst Internship requires proficiency in Power BI for creating advanced dashboards and visualizations, basic to intermediate knowledge of SQL for database management and querying, and strong analytical and organizational skills. The Statistical Quality Data Scientist Internship requires strong statistical and analytical skills, proficiency in Power BI for creating sophisticated dashboards and visualizations, experience in data transformation and processing, particularly with Excel and large datasets, and familiarity with statistical modeling and predictive analytics techniques, including Weibull Analysis. The AI/ML Acoustic Diagnostics Internship offers hands-on experience with real-world Industry 4.0 projects in a manufacturing environment, mentorship from experienced professionals in data analytics and manufacturing, exposure to cutting-edge technologies and tools for digital transformation, and networking opportunities and potential for a full-time role upon successful completion of the internship.

Company Overview: The Building Management System (BMS) Analytics Challenge is a data science competition where participants are required to perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) and build an interactive visual dashboard using Google Data Studio to gain insights from a given dataset related to building maintenance and operations. The dataset consists of records about facilities, equipment, maintenance tasks, technicians, and timestamps. The goal is to analyze trends, identify patterns, and optimize building maintenance operations by applying clustering techniques and time series analysis. The submission includes a link to the dashboard, an EDA report, and a submission form. The evaluation criteria include data cleaning and preprocessing, EDA, predictive modelling, and dashboard design and interactivity.

Role Overview: In this role, you will be tasked with performing exploratory data analysis (EDA) and developing a fully interactive visual dashboard using "google data studio (looker’s studio)" to visualize key insights from the given Building Management System (BMS) dataset. Your goal is to analyze trends, identify patterns, and uncover insights that can help in optimizing building maintenance operations. Additionally, you can apply clustering techniques for data segmentation and conduct time series analysis to identify trends and forecast maintenance needs.

Here it is: "The dataset consists of multiple fields capturing critical maintenance-related information: • Task_ID – Unique identifier for each maintenance task. • Equipment_Code – Code representing the equipment being serviced. • Facility_Name – Name of the facility where maintenance is performed. • System_Class – Type or classification of the system/equipment. • Service_Tag – Identifier for the service order related to the task. • Error_Code – Fault/error code recorded during maintenance. • Inspection_Type – Indicates the type of inspection conducted. • Maintenance_Type – Specifies whether the task is preventive or corrective. • Project_Stage – The stage of the maintenance project (e.g., scheduled, ongoing, completed). • Work_Order – The reference number for the work order associated with the task. • Urgency_Level – Priority level assigned to the task (e.g., low, medium, high). • Site_Reference – Identifier for the specific site location. • Planner_ID – ID of the person responsible for planning the maintenance. • Work_Hours – The number of hours spent on the task. • Task_Category – Category/classification of the maintenance task. • Technician – The technician assigned to complete the maintenance. • Technician_Role – Role of the technician (e.g., senior engineer, junior technician). • Task_Description – A brief description of the maintenance task. • Job_Status – Current status of the job (e.g., pending, in progress, completed). • Completion_Time – Timestamp when the task was completed. • Request_Time – Timestamp when the task was requested. • Start_Time – Timestamp when the task was started. • System_Timestamp – System-generated timestamp recording the event."

Selection Process The selection process for the Building Management System (BMS) Analytics Challenge consists of the following stages: 1. Data Cleaning and Preprocessing: In this stage, participants will be required to clean and preprocess the dataset to ensure data quality and consistency. This may include handling missing values, outliers, and inconsistencies, as well as converting timestamps into meaningful time-based features. 2. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA): Participants will perform EDA to gain insights into the dataset and identify trends, patterns, and correlations. This may involve data visualization techniques, statistical analysis, and hypothesis testing. 3. Clustering Analysis (if applicable): Participants may choose to apply clustering techniques to segment the data into meaningful groups based on similarities. 4. Time Series Analysis (if applicable): Participants may conduct time series analysis to identify trends and forecast maintenance needs. 5. Predictive Modelling: Participants may develop predictive models to forecast maintenance needs or identify potential issues before they occur. 6. Dashboard Design and Interactivity: Participants will design and build an interactive dashboard using Google Data Studio (Looker’s Studio) to visualize key insights from their analysis. 7. Submission: Participants will submit their EDA report, interactive dashboard, and predictions for the test dataset. 8. Evaluation: Submissions will be evaluated based on the following criteria: - Data Cleaning & Preprocessing (20%) - Exploratory Data Analysis (40%) - Predictive Modelling (10%) - Dashboard Design & Interactivity (30%) The evaluation criteria weightages may vary slightly depending on the specific challenge requirements.

Company Overview: Beckman Coulter is a global leader in life science research and clinical diagnostics, delivering solutions that answer questions in biology with certainty. With a multifaceted and integrated approach, Beckman Coulter develops, manufactures, and markets diagnostic systems, instruments, reagents, and assays that help customers achieve both research and diagnostic goals. The company's products touch nearly every aspect of modern healthcare, enabling laboratories to provide better patient care, faster diagnosis, and improved patient outcomes every day. Beckman Coulter's diagnostic solutions include automated systems for chemistry, immunoassay, hematology, and molecular diagnostics, as well as manual systems for serology, hematology, and urinalysis. The company's research solutions include instruments for protein purification, sample preparation, and mass spectrometry, as well as reagents and assays for protein and peptide analysis, gene expression, and epigenetics. With a focus on innovation, Beckman Coulter invests in research and development to create products that meet the evolving needs of its customers. The company's commitment to quality and reliability is reflected in its ISO 13485 certification, which ensures that its products meet the highest standards for medical devices. Role Overview: You are responsible for designing, developing and maintaining Diagnostic X-rays (Radiography, Fluoro & Mammography) and ensuring that future product options and features can be realized with acceptable effects for earlier products. You will identify appropriate technologies for overall system, sub-system and component levels and ensure that your design is consistent with the higher-level architecture and requirements. You will create the mechanical and electrical requirements, ensuring the mutual consistency and efficient integration of the separate modules, and develop electro-mechanical (mechatronics) products by studying customer requirements, researching, and testing manufacturing and assembly methods. You will analyze product(s) issues from the field (post-market) and take complete ownership in providing a relevant solution(s). You will define subsystem architecture under considerations of platform constraints and work closely with all the other team members both R&D team members and cross-functional stakeholders for the projects. You will work closely with local and global suppliers and ensure reliability and serviceability of the system/subsystem/components. You will transfer design output to the manufacturing line and advise on realization, planning, and budgets. You will contribute to Technology Roadmaps and perform work in line with the processes that have been agreed upon in the department. You will maintain product and company reputation by complying with country-specific regulations. To succeed in this role, you should have a MTech/B.E/BTech in Mechanical engineering or Mechatronics Engineering and 10+ years of relevant experience in design & development of system/subsystem. You should have expertise in design techniques, application domain, and advanced engineering including model-based design. You should be able to technically lead a team with domain expertise in Machine Design, motion control engineering, Manufacturing process, Materials selection, Value Engineering. You should be analytical, creative, and intellectual thinker, with the ability to perform electro-mechanical design calculations & verify designs. You should have knowledge of structural design, CAD (Creating 3D models and manufacturing drawings), FEM analysis, and be good at hand calculation of the design. You should create DFMEA and identify relevant product risks. You should identify the suitable material, create EBOM, DFM/DFA, and have experience in the latest engineering design and methods, tools, and processes. You should create functional requirement documentation and have knowledge of IEC 60601 standards
